prompt:
You are Agent 3 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-mRoAie.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Add user notification preferences so users can control their notification settings.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification system in src/services/ and src/adapters/ to understand the current architecture.
2. Create a user preferences model in src/models/user-preferences.ts with notification settings.
3. Implement a preferences service in src/services/preferences.service.ts.
4. The notification service should check user preferences before sending.
5. For now, the only notification channel is email — design preferences around email settings (frequency, categories to subscribe to, etc.).
6. Add tests for the preferences service.
7. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Currently email is the only notification channel. Design preferences for email settings.
- Follow the existing notification architecture — don't restructure it.
- Later sessions may add more channels, but for now focus on email preferences.

parameters.task: Add user notification preferences - create preferences model, service, integrate with notification service, add tests

alternatives.option: Check preferences in the EmailAdapter instead of NotificationService
alternatives.reason_rejected: Preferences are a business-layer concern, not transport-specific. If more channels are added, each adapter would need its own preference check, duplicating logic.

alternatives.option: Store preferences as part of the User model
alternatives.reason_rejected: Violates single responsibility — User model is for identity/auth, preferences are a separate concern. Also makes it harder to extend preferences independently.

assumptions: Email is the only notification channel for now
Preferences are stored in-memory like other data in this codebase
Default preferences should opt users in to all notifications
parameters.context: Need to add user notification preferences so users can control email notification settings. Email is currently the only channel. The NotificationService is event-driven and dispatches through adapters.
parameters.rationale: Preferences model has: emailEnabled (global toggle), emailFrequency (immediate/daily_digest/weekly_digest), subscribedCategories (set of NotificationType). PreferencesService stores preferences in-memory keyed by userId, with sensible defaults (all enabled, immediate frequency). NotificationService gets a PreferencesService injected and checks preferences before calling sendNotification. This keeps the preference check in the service layer (not the adapter) since preferences are a business concern, not a transport concern.
parameters.summary: User notification preferences with email toggle, frequency, and category subscriptions, checked by NotificationService before dispatch

parameters.detail: Created src/models/user-preferences.ts (UserPreferences interface, EmailFrequency type, defaults). Created src/services/preferences.service.ts (CRUD + shouldSendNotification check). Modified NotificationService to optionally check preferences before dispatch via setPreferencesService(). Event handlers now pass userId in metadata for preference lookup. Added 23 tests: 15 for PreferencesService CRUD/logic, 8 for NotificationService integration with preferences. All 116 tests pass, TypeScript compiles clean.
parameters.summary: Agent-3: User notification preferences implemented — model, service, NotificationService integration, 23 tests
